pi_items.source: Task description: 'Set the Number of executors to 1'. Verified against Jenkins API documentation - numExecutors is a numeric field representing the number of concurrent build executors on a node.

Jenkins API documentation (javadoc.jenkins.io/hudson/model/Node.Mode.html) confirms two valid Node.Mode enum values: NORMAL and EXCLUSIVE. Task description step 4: 'Change the Usage to Only build jobs with label expressions matching this node' maps to EXCLUSIVE mode. Verified through verifier.py line 88 which checks: if mode == 'EXCLUSIVE'.

Task description step 3 explicitly states: 'Set the Labels field to: controller lightweight'. Verified against Jenkins API documentation showing assignedLabels as array of objects with 'name' property. Verifier.py lines 72-76 confirm both labels must be present (case-insensitive).
Task description step 6 explicitly states: 'Create a new Freestyle project named controller-health-check'. Verified in verifier.py line 29 and export_result.sh line 29.
Task description step 7 explicitly states: 'set the Label Expression to: controller'. Verified in verifier.py line 129 which checks: if 'controller' in label_expr.

Task description step 8 specifies shell command: 'echo "Health check running on controller node" && hostname && date && uptime'. This contains both required substrings. Verified in verifier.py lines 148-149 which checks for 'health check' or 'health_check' (case-insensitive) in the command. 'hostname' is explicit in the task description command.

privileged_info_summary: The Jenkins built-in node (controller) must be configured with exactly 1 executor to handle only label-restricted jobs. The node should be labeled with both 'controller' and 'lightweight' to prevent heavy builds from running on the controller itself. A test job named 'controller-health-check' should be created as a Freestyle project restricted to the 'controller' label with a shell script containing a health check command and hostname information. The node mode must be set to 'EXCLUSIVE' to enforce the label restriction policy.
